reason: The sole residual finding concerns a class-level closing `}` added to the s9 code example. The source RST code block is syntactically incomplete Java (the class declaration opens but never closes). The knowledge file's addition of the closing brace makes the code snippet syntactically valid and directly usable by developers. Removing it to match the source would produce broken, non-compilable code. The "fabrication" label is technically accurate but the change is strictly beneficial — no user is harmed or misled. Phase E attempted to remove the brace but the output was truncated mid-JSON (visible at the end of the Phase E log), so the fix was never applied; however, since the net effect on users is neutral-to-positive, no further intervention is warranted.

findings_assessment.justification: The added `}` closes the class declaration that the source RST left syntactically open. Java code without a class-closing brace does not compile; the knowledge file's version is more correct and directly usable. No misleading or incorrect information is introduced — the only deviation from source is completing an obviously incomplete code block. Phase E did not resolve this because its JSON output was truncated mid-string, leaving the section unchanged, but the user-facing effect of the current content is neutral-to-positive.
